MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-26-2018 Set For Trial On Oct-07-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Oppose Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DANTE DALPORTO'S Motion To Oppose Good Faith Settlement Determination.

Defendant Dante Dalporto's motion contesting Defendants Molly Alsnauer and Erick Perez's application for good faith settlement determination is denied, and Defendants' application for a good faith settlement determination is granted. Mr. Perez has agreed to pay $15,000 to Plaintiff Nicholas Renault, the entire amount of his available liability insurance coverage, and his declaration shows that he has exceedingly modest assets and is effectively judgment proof. (Perez Decl. 3-6; see Dole Food Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 894, 909 [relevant considerations include the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["Notwithstanding the settlement's disproportionality, because Guerrero lacks other assets, substantial evidence supports the finding of good faith settlement"].) Under the circumstances, Mr. Dalporto fails to meet his burden to show that the settlement is not in good faith. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d) [the party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue]; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Mr. Dalporto's contention that the motion is "premature" because discovery has just commenced lacks merit. The litigation has been pending for over one year, since June 2018. In any event, a good faith determination may be sought even before litigation is filed. (County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero, 209 Cal.App.3d at 1155 ["the fact that the instant settlement was entered into early does not preclude a finding of good faith"].)

Counsel for the Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
